_note: Image discovery queue. Engines (see RESEARCH_PLAN.md) append rows; the convergence loop advances status: proposed -> verified -> sourced -> cataloged (or rights_blocked). period = by SUBJECT. via: direct|reception|edition. rights: pd|cc|in_copyright|unknown. This is a SEED across all gaps; agents expand it. build_db.py can ingest into a `wanted` table for gap reporting.

wanted.title: Initiation of Lucius into the Mysteries of Isis (Golden Ass)
wanted.subject: apuleius_golden_ass
wanted.why: Antique mystery-cult image-source; no ancient illustration survives — trace Isis iconography + illustrated editions.

wanted.title: Cupid and Psyche cycle (from the Golden Ass)
wanted.subject: apuleius_golden_ass
wanted.why: The Neoplatonic soul-allegory embedded in Apuleius; vast art-historical afterlife (Raphael, Coypel).

wanted.title: Map obrist_medieval dump to Obrist's catalogue of early alchemical imagery
wanted.subject: early_alchemical_imagery
wanted.why: We hold an unlabeled page-dump; Obrist's plate-list is the finite spec for medieval alchemical images.

candidate_sources.note: harvest her figure-list, then source high-res per MS

wanted.title: Aurora Consurgens — complete Zürich miniature cycle (high-res)
wanted.subject: aurora_consurgens
wanted.why: We have a partial set; e-codices has the full Ms. Rh. 172 at IIIF resolution.

wanted.title: Ripley Scroll — the major surviving witnesses
wanted.subject: ripley_scroll
wanted.why: Several spectacular scrolls survive (Wellcome, Huntington, BL, Getty); catalog the image, list witnesses.

wanted.title: Geheime Figuren der Rosenkreuzer (Secret Symbols of the Rosicrucians) — full plates
wanted.subject: rosicrucian_symbols
wanted.why: The keystone Rosicrucian colour-plate corpus (Altona, 1785–88); iconic and public domain.

wanted.title: Fludd — the Divine Monochord
wanted.subject: fludd_cosmology
wanted.why: Iconic macrocosm-as-instrument diagram; pairs with the Great Chain we already hold.

wanted.title: Khunrath — the Oratory-Laboratory plate
wanted.subject: khunrath_amphitheatrum
wanted.why: The most famous Khunrath engraving; identify it within our khunrath page-scans or source clean.

wanted.title: Hans Baldung Grien — Witches' Sabbath (1510 chiaroscuro woodcut)
wanted.subject: witchcraft
wanted.why: Foundational image of the witchcraft visual tradition.

wanted.title: Compendium Maleficarum (Guazzo, 1608) — the demonological woodcuts
wanted.subject: witchcraft
wanted.why: The canonical sabbath/pact/flight woodcut suite.

wanted.title: Newes from Scotland (1591) — witch-trial pamphlet woodcuts
wanted.subject: witchcraft
wanted.why: Early printed witch-trial propaganda imagery.

wanted.title: Dictionnaire Infernal (Collin de Plancy, 1863) — demon portraits
wanted.subject: demonology
wanted.why: The famous illustrated demon gallery (Bael, Astaroth, etc.); pairs with our Goetia data.

wanted.title: The 72 Goetia seals — whole-page plates (Mathers/Crowley 1904)
wanted.subject: goetia
wanted.why: Turns the Goetic Hierarchy collection live; cross-link our GoetiaRevEng analysis.

wanted.title: The Witch of Endor raising Samuel — across art history
wanted.subject: biblical_magic
wanted.why: Necromancy in canonical art (West, Rosa, Martynov…); a whole iconographic class.

wanted.title: The Fall of Simon Magus — across art history
wanted.subject: biblical_magic
wanted.why: The arch-magician of Christian legend; Romanesque→Baroque depictions.

wanted.title: Moses & Aaron vs Pharaoh's magicians (serpent-rods)
wanted.subject: biblical_magic
wanted.why: The serpent/transformation contest; read alchemically in the period.

wanted.title: The Adoration of the Magi (as astrologers following the star)
wanted.subject: biblical_magic
wanted.why: The Magi as astrologer-kings; vast PD corpus tied to astrology.

wanted.title: Gnostic / Abraxas magical gems
wanted.subject: magical_gems
wanted.why: Genuine antique occult objects (Abraxas, Chnoubis); museum-photographed.

candidate_sources.where: British Museum / The Met / CBd (Campbell Bonner Magical Gems DB)

wanted.title: Greek Magical Papyri (PGM) — voces magicae & charakteres drawings
wanted.subject: pgm
wanted.why: The drawn sigils/charakteres of late-antique ritual magic.

wanted.title: Mithraic tauroctony & astral imagery
wanted.subject: mithraism
wanted.why: Late-antique mystery cult with explicit astral symbolism.

wanted.title: Visconti-Sforza tarot (15th c.) — the surviving cards
wanted.subject: tarot
wanted.why: The earliest substantial tarot deck; origin of the divination-image lineage.

wanted.title: Tarot de Marseille (Conver, 1760) — full deck
wanted.subject: tarot
wanted.why: The canonical historical deck.

wanted.title: Rider-Waite-Smith tarot (1909) — full deck
wanted.subject: tarot
wanted.why: The most influential modern deck; now public domain (pre-1929).

wanted.title: The Children of the Planets (Hausbuch / blockbook series)
wanted.subject: astrology
wanted.why: The classic series pairing each planet with the human types it governs.

wanted.title: The Zodiac Man / Homo Signorum (Books of Hours, almanacs)
wanted.subject: astrology
wanted.why: Astrological medicine's signature image across centuries.

wanted.title: Portae Lucis (1516) frontispiece — the Sephirotic Tree
wanted.subject: kabbalah
wanted.why: The first printed Tree of Life in a Christian book; the keystone Kabbalah image.

wanted.title: Kircher — Sephirotic Tree (Oedipus Aegyptiacus)
wanted.subject: kabbalah
wanted.why: The famous baroque Tree of Life engraving.

wanted.title: John Dee — the Monas Hieroglyphica glyph & diagrams

wanted.title: John Dee — Sigillum Dei Aemeth
wanted.subject: enochian
wanted.why: The great angelic seal (BL Sloane MS).

wanted.title: Dürer — Melencolia I (1514)
wanted.subject: saturn_melancholy
wanted.why: The supreme image of Saturnine melancholy, the magic square, and occult geometry.

wanted.title: Éliphas Lévi — Baphomet (the Sabbatic Goat, 1856)
wanted.subject: occult_revival
wanted.why: The defining image of the 19th-c. occult revival.

wanted.title: Besant & Leadbeater — Thought-Forms (1901) plates
wanted.subject: theosophy
wanted.why: Proto-abstract Theosophical colour plates; hugely influential.

wanted.title: Gichtel — the Theosophical Man (Boehme tradition)
wanted.subject: boehmenism
wanted.why: The chakra-like diagram of the regenerate soul.

wanted.title: Gratheus, Introduction to Alchemy — picture cycle (Ylarius/Virgo/Multipos)

wanted.title: Aurora consurgens — full 37-illustration cycle
wanted.subject: aurora_consurgens
wanted.why: Obrist Visualization fig.11; richest medieval alchemical picture-cycle. Zurich Rh.172, fully on e-codices.

wanted.title: Alembic apparatus (BnF lat. 6514)
wanted.subject: alchemical_apparatus
wanted.why: Obrist Visualization fig.7; late-13th-c. apparatus. BnF lat.6514 fol.70r on Gallica.

wanted.title: Constantine of Pisa, Book of the Secrets of Alchemy
wanted.subject: constantine_of_pisa
wanted.why: Obrist Visualization fig.4; earliest dated (1361) creation-of-metals programme. Glasgow Ferg.104 fol.45v.

wanted.title: Semita recta — vessels & furnace
wanted.subject: pseudo_albertus
wanted.why: Obrist Visualization fig.8; early depictions of processes in glass vessels. Glasgow Hunt.110.

wanted.title: Buch der heiligen Dreifaltigkeit — illustrated cycle
wanted.subject: book_of_holy_trinity
wanted.why: Obrist Visualization fig.2; first richly illustrated German alchemical work, ca.1420. GNM Nurnberg ms.80061; Berlin SBB.

wanted.title: Pseudo-Lull, Testamentum — rotation of the elements
wanted.subject: pseudo_lull
wanted.why: Obrist Visualization fig.15; elemental-rotation diagram. Oxford CCC 244 fol.58vb.

wanted.title: The Ripley Scroll — emblematic alchemical verse-scroll
wanted.subject: george_ripley
wanted.why: Discussed by Obrist as terminal case where image overwhelms text; many 15-16c copies (BL, Bodleian, Yale, Getty).

candidate_sources.note: numerous copies; choose a digitized PD one

wanted.title: Three Worlds / 32 Paths of Wisdom diagram (Boderie, Heptaple 1578)
wanted.subject: christian_cabala
wanted.why: The Crofton Black Pico-Heptaplus cover image: Nicolas Le Fevre de la Boderie, Le coeur Leb ou les 32 sentiers de sapience, in the 1578 French Heptaple. Not on Commons; PD print on Gallica.

candidate_sources.note: ark:/12148/bpt6k8584280 (LHarmonie du monde + lHeptaple); locate the 32-sentiers diagram folio, pull via IIIF
